channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: At 89, Fred Costello plays the organ on opening day in Rochester just like he has since 1977
item.description: Fred Costello could swing a bat about as well as he could perform a melody as a kid growing up in Syracuse, New York. He wanted to play at ballparks, and figured the best way to reach that dream was through slick fielding and a sweet swing.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LPGA Tour takes center stage in Las Vegas as PGA Tour plots future return
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Reigning Player of the Year and world No. 1 Nelly Korda is back in the Las Vegas area this week to defend her T-Mobile Match Play title.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for cycle in win over the Athletics
item.description: Chicago Cubs catcher Carson Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle in the team's 18-3 rout over the Athletics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA suspends 5 players for their roles in Pistons-Timberwolves altercation that spilled into stands
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Detroit's Isaiah Stewart was suspended for two games and four other players from the Pistons and Minnesota Timberwolves drew one-game suspensions for their roles in an on-court altercation between the teams earlier this week, the NBA said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No stigma for Bueckers, Van Lith or Fudd. The basketball stars seek out mental health professionals
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Paige Bueckers, Hailey Van Lith and Azzi Fudd all have reached out to a mental health professional for help at one point, seeking assistance to cope with the increasing pressure on college basketball players.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Top takeaways from 49ers coach Kyle Shanahan’s first public comments since January
item.description: Kyle Shanahan, three months into the 49ers' offseason overhaul, met with reporters Tuesday morning at the NFL owners meetings in Palm Beach, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Padres pick up another win, making for single best start to season ever
item.description: The San Diego Padres have made history after beating the Cleveland Guardians 7-2 at Petco Park on Monday, giving them a five-game winning streak to start the season for the first time ever.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: US-Canada-Mexico joint World Cup goes from unity to acrimony thanks to tariffs and '51st state' talk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Seven years ago, when a joint bid by the United States, Canada and Mexico was awarded the 2026 World Cup, rifts created by tariffs — yes, back then, too! — and a proposed border wall were glossed over because of the neighbors’ longstanding political and economic alliances.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A's routed by Cubs 18-3 in Sacramento home opener
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) -- Carson Kelly hit for the cycle and drove in five runs, Michael Busch launched a two-run homer on the way to a career-high four RBIs, and the Chicago Cubs pounded the Athletics 18-3 on Monday night to spoil their first game in their new Northern California home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Paige Bueckers carries UConn to record 24th women's Final Four with 78-64 win over USC
item.description: SPOKANE, Wash. (AP) — Paige Bueckers and her UConn teammates didn’t cut down the net after securing a trip to the Final Four, even though a ladder was set up and ready to go. The reason was simple: The Huskies aren’t done yet.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame guard Olivia Miles to enter transfer portal instead of WNBA draft, AP source says
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Olivia Miles has decided to enter the transfer portal and play another college season instead of going to the WNBA, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Celtics remain at home on the road, now 2 wins from tying NBA mark for away victories
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — The best road trip in Boston history has given the Celtics a chance at grabbing a piece of NBA history. The Celtics finished off the franchise's first 6-0 road trip on Monday night with a 117-103 win at Memphis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Celtics beat Grizzlies 117-103 and complete the first 6-0 trip in franchise history
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Jayson Tatum had 25 points and 14 rebounds, Al Horford scored 26 points and the Boston Celtics swept a six-game road trip for the first time in franchise history with a 117-103 win over the Memphis Grizzlies on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Garrett Crochet agrees to $170 million, 6-year deal with Red Sox
item.description: Ace left-hander Garrett Crochet has agreed to a record-breaking $170 million, six-year contract with the Boston Red Sox.
item.dc:creator: Jay Cohen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NFL defensive lineman John Cominsky retires at 29 due to injuries
item.description: Former NFL defensive lineman John Cominsky announced he was retiring from the league after five seasons due to "overwhelming" injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Milwaukee pays tribute to Bob Uecker in Brewers' first home game since his death
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — As the Milwaukee Brewers conducted a moment of silence before their home opener to honor Bob Uecker, one fan decided keeping quiet wasn’t the best way to pay tribute to the lovable loquacious broadcaster. “We love you, Ueck,” the spectator shouted.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utes lose to Butler in College Basktball Crown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - The Runnin' Utes got one more opportunity to play this season, but lost to Butler in the inaugural College Basketball Crown tournament in Las Vegas Monday, 86-84. Keanu Dawes, who entered the transfer portal last week, led the Utes with 19 points and 11 rebounds.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Stanford hires Frank Reich as interim football coach
item.description: STANFORD, Calif. (AP) — Andrew Luck picked a familiar face to take over as Stanford's interim football coach, hiring former NFL coach Frank Reich on Monday to replace the fired Troy Taylor.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine school officials explain why state was in 'noncompliance with Title IX' after referral to DOJ
item.description: Maine school officials explained why they were unable to sign the Trump administration's agreement to keep trans athletes out of girls' and women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk 'deserves all the accolades that you can give him,' NFL legend Brett Favre says
item.description: NFL legend Brett Favre talked about the attacks on billionaire Elon Musk as he took over as senior advisor to President Donald Trump in the last two months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2025 NASCAR Chicago Street Race: City, NASCAR announce traffic plan for before, during, after race weekend
item.description: CHICAGO — The City of Chicago and NASCAR officials have announced the traffic plan for the third NASCAR Chicago Street Race, scheduled for July 5-6 (Saturday and Sunday) in Grant Park.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Amalie Arena transforms for NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: Amalie Arena is all about basketball for the next week, as the NCAA Women's Final Four takes over Tampa with events around the city.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suns star Kevin Durant helped off court after injuring left leg
item.description: Phoenix Suns All-Star Kevin Durant was helped to the locker room with 6:57 left in the third quarter against Houston on Sunday night after falling to the floor in a collision with the Rockets' Jabari Smith Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Royals' Jonathan India hit by 99 mph fastball, leaves game
item.description: "He threw a pitch that he doesn't ever throw, the pitch before, which was a four-seam fastball. It was his first one of the year," Pasquantino said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, NewsNation

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Duke and Florida move on to Final Four
item.description: Florida and Duke won their regions on Saturday, with fellow top seeds Houston and Auburn set to play on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, NewsNation

item.title: New San Diego Padres mural in North Park nears completion
item.description: A new San Diego Padres-themed mural in North Park replacing a previous work of public art that had been defaced earlier this year by fans of the rival Los Angeles Dodgers is nearing completion, just in time for opening weekend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Runnin' Utes name Chris Wilcox general manager
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah head men's basketball coach Alex Jensen continues to fill his staff, announcing the addition of Sacramento Kings Assistant GM Wes Wilcox as the newest general manager for the Runnin' Utes program.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rays prepare for Tampa home opener
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Tampa Bay Rays have their home opener for their season at Steinbrenner Field on Friday. The game is already a sell out. Seating capacity at this years games will be greatly reduced, due to major hurricane damage at Tropicana Field.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rail service to and from Petco Park expands for Padres season
item.description: The North County Transit District is expanding COASTER service to provide fans with a safe and convenient way to get to and from Petco Park on Padres game days.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5
